days.title: May 7 — Arrival · Piccadilly & the Palace
days.note: Arriving noon · afternoon tea booked · Duck & Waffle reservation

stops.name: Buckingham Palace & Victoria Memorial
stops.travel: 🚶 Walk from hotel through Green Park.
stops.history: Buckingham Palace has been the official London residence of the monarch since 1837. The Victoria Memorial is crowned by a gilded figure of Victory with a seated Queen Victoria below.
stops.todo: Walk along The Mall toward the palace gates — one of London's great vistas.

stops.name: Fortnum & Mason — Diamond Jubilee Tea Salon
stops.history: Fortnum & Mason has stood on Piccadilly since 1707. The Diamond Jubilee Tea Salon on the fourth floor was created for the Queen's Diamond Jubilee in 2012 — one of London's most celebrated afternoon tea destinations.
stops.todo: 🫖 Full afternoon tea with signature pale turquoise china, three-tier stands of finger sandwiches, scones with clotted cream, and exquisite pastries.

stops.name: Fortnum & Mason — Browse the Food Halls
stops.history: The ground floor food halls are a feast — hampers, jams, chocolates, and the famous confectionery counter with hand-made marzipan fruits.
stops.todo: Browse the confectionery counter and pick up a tin of Fortnum's tea.

stops.name: Hatchards Bookshop
stops.history: Hatchards was founded in 1797 and is London's oldest bookshop. Royal warrant holder since 1801.
stops.todo: A passed-by gem on Piccadilly.

stops.name: Duck & Waffle — Rooftop Dinner
stops.travel: 🚇 Take the Tube or taxi to Liverpool Street / Bishopsgate.
stops.history: Duck & Waffle is London's highest restaurant, on the 40th floor of Heron Tower, open 24 hours with panoramic views of the entire city. Named for its signature dish of crispy duck leg confit on a waffle with a fried duck egg.
stops.todo: 🦆Order the signature Duck & Waffle. Book the window table if possible. The views at night are extraordinary — you can see for miles across London.

stops.name: West End Theater Pub
stops.history: A classic London theater pub in the West End, walls covered in original musical theater posters including Oklahoma!, with a lively atmosphere after shows.
stops.todo: The perfect end to a West End evening.

days.title: May 8 — Westminster · V&A · Sky Garden
days.note: Full day across Westminster, South Kensington and the City

stops.name: The Wolseley — Breakfast
stops.history: The Wolseley opened in 2003 in a 1920s palace built for the Wolseley Motor Company. The soaring arched ceiling, marble floors and gilded ironwork make breakfast here one of London's great experiences.
stops.todo: 🥐 Order the Full Breakfast or Eggs Benedict with smoked salmon. Sit in the main room.

stops.name: Westminster Abbey
stops.travel: 🚶 15-min walk from The Wolseley down St James's Park.
stops.history: Westminster Abbey has been the coronation church since 1066. The carved Gothic portal depicting the Last Judgement is one of the finest in England.
stops.todo: 👑 Look for Poets' Corner and the Coronation Chair where monarchs have been crowned since 1308.

stops.name: V&A Museum — Cast Courts & Gardens
stops.travel: 🚶 Walk from Westminster through Victoria to South Kensington.
stops.history: The V&A Cast Courts house one of the world's greatest collections of plaster casts — including a full-scale replica of Trajan's Column and Michelangelo's David. The John Madejski Garden in the centre is one of London's hidden peaceful courtyards.
stops.todo: 🏛️ Find Trajan's Column split across two rooms. The garden café is perfect for a break.

stops.name: V&A Museum Café — The Gamble Room
stops.history: The Gamble Room is one of the world's first museum restaurants, designed by James Gamble in 1868. The extraordinary domed ceiling, stained glass windows, and decorated tile columns are breathtaking.
stops.todo: Coffee and cake under the extraordinary dome. One of London's most beautiful rooms.

stops.name: Sky Garden — Dinner at the Top
stops.travel: 🚇 Tube to Monument or Bank, then walk.
stops.history: Sky Garden occupies the top three floors of 20 Fenchurch Street (the Walkie-Talkie building), London's most unusual skyscraper. The glass-enclosed garden with subtropical plants offers 360° panoramic views including The Shard, St Paul's, and the Thames.
stops.todo: 🌿 Book the Francis Golding outdoor terrace for sunset views. The Shard is directly to the south. The garden is extraordinary at dusk.

stops.name: Sketch — Breakfast in The Glade
stops.history: Sketch is one of London's most extraordinary restaurants in an 18th-century Mayfair townhouse. The Glade is an enchanting woodland room with floor-to-ceiling forest murals, trees hanging from the ceiling, and golden flower lamps — like breakfast in a fairy-tale forest.
stops.todo: 🌿 The forest murals cover every surface. Order the pastry basket and fresh juices.

stops.name: Leicester Square — Swiss Glockenspiel
stops.history: The Swiss Glockenspiel clock on Leicester Square was a gift from Switzerland featuring all 26 cantonal flags with bell-ringing figurines. The M&M's World store and street artists make the square always lively.
stops.todo: The clock chimes on the hour. Leicester Square is always buzzing.

stops.name: Chinatown — Gerrard Street
stops.history: London's Chinatown has been centred on Gerrard Street since the 1970s. The ceremonial gates were a gift from the Chinese government. Red lanterns hang along the pedestrian street year-round.
stops.todo: Try the BBQ pork buns from a bakery. Walk under the red lanterns the full length of Gerrard Street.

stops.name: Covent Garden Market
stops.history: Covent Garden Market has traded since the 1650s. The current building opened in 1830 and was converted into a shopping and entertainment piazza in 1980.
stops.todo: Watch the street performers in the piazza. The Apple Market has crafts and antiques.

stops.name: Rules Restaurant
stops.history: Rules is London's oldest restaurant, established in 1798. For over 225 years it has served traditional British food in rooms hung with antlers, cartoons, and theatrical portraits. Charles Dickens dined here regularly.
stops.todo: 🦌 Order the game dishes — venison, grouse, or the legendary steak and kidney pudding. Study the extraordinary portraits and antlers.

stops.name: Caffè Concerto
stops.history: Caffè Concerto is an elegant London café group known for its ornate crystal chandeliers, fresh pink flowers on every table, and beautifully presented cakes and light meals.
stops.todo: 🌹 The crystal chandeliers and fresh flowers make it wonderfully special. Try the signature berry cakes.

stops.name: BBC Broadcasting House
stops.travel: 🚶 Walk through Regent Street / Marylebone area.
stops.history: BBC Broadcasting House has been the home of the BBC since 1932. The circular addition glows brilliant blue at night — one of London's most photogenic buildings after dark.
stops.todo: A beautiful sight at night with the blue-lit circular extension glowing.

days.title: May 10 — Borough · The City · Thames Cruise
days.note: Farewell day · Thames Dinner Cruise booked 7:45 PM

stops.name: Borough Market — Breakfast
stops.history: Borough Market has existed in some form for over 1,000 years and is London's most celebrated food market. Over 100 stalls sell everything from fresh produce to street food from around the world.
stops.todo: 🥐 Try the Raclette cheese stall, the Neal's Yard cheese counter, and get a coffee from Monmouth. Arrive early — it gets very busy by 10am.

stops.name: The Shard Viewing Gallery
stops.history: The Shard was designed by Renzo Piano and completed in 2012 — at 310 metres it is Western Europe's tallest building. The viewing gallery on floors 68-72 gives a 360° panorama of London on a clear day.
stops.todo: 🏙️ Try to go on a clear morning. The views of Tower Bridge, the City, and the Thames are extraordinary.

stops.name: Leadenhall Market
stops.history: Leadenhall Market was built in 1881 by Sir Horace Jones (who also designed Tower Bridge) and is one of London's oldest markets, dating to the 14th century. The ornate Victorian cast-iron roof with its burgundy, green and gold paintwork is magnificent. Used as Diagon Alley in Harry Potter.
stops.todo: Look up at the extraordinary painted vaulted ceiling. The pubs and wine bars are excellent for a lunchtime stop.

stops.name: Dinner Cruise on the River Thames
stops.address: Thames River, London — check GetYourGuide for boarding point
stops.travel: 🚢 Check GetYourGuide app for exact boarding location and time.
stops.history: A candlelit dinner cruise along the River Thames — one of London's most romantic experiences. Glide past illuminated landmarks: the Houses of Parliament, Tower Bridge, St Paul's Cathedral, and the Tate Modern as the city lights up after dark.
stops.todo: 🛳️Booked — $283 for 2 adults. Tickets in GetYourGuide app. Dress smartly. Arrive 15 min early. Watch for Tower Bridge lit up as you pass underneath at night — unforgettable.
stops.prebookText: Booked via GetYourGuide — mobile tickets ready
